name: Artichokes Alla Giudea
recipeInstructions:
1. Pull off the outer layers of deep green leaves from each artichoke and discard.
2. Cut off the stem and set aside. Rub each cut with a lemon wedge.
3. Place each artichoke on its side and cut off the top third. Discard these points.
4. Using a pair of scissors, cut off the sharp tips.
5. Soak the artichokes for 10 minutes in cold water mixed with juice from 1 ounce of lemon.
6. Carefully open the leaves by prying with your fingers as if opening a flower and pressing down on the counter, making sure to separate each leaf and season all over with salt and pepper.
7. Heat the virgin olive oil in a tall skillet or saucepan (8-inch to 10-inch wide at the base) until it just begins to smoke.
8. Cook the artichokes in the hot oil for 12 to 15 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are golden all over.
9. After 10 minutes of cooking, gently shake your wet hand over the artichokes to mist them with oil; be careful as this will cause the oil to sizzle and spatter.
10. When the artichokes are cooked, remove each one by skewering its base with a fork. Press the flower side down to force it further open.
11. Drain on paper towels, season again with salt and pepper, and serve with lemon wedges.
